items.title: Recriminalising meth ignores evidence, drug harm advocates warn

children.content: Drug harm reduction advocates says there is little evidence that methamphetamine is more harmful in the ACT since being decriminalised, despite a Canberra Liberals commitment to recriminalise it if elected.

gallery.altText: Three large white crystals on a brown tray with a piece of foil nearby.

thumbnail.altText: Three large white crystals on a brown tray with a piece of foil nearby.

items.title: Paramedic 'haunted' by woman's death at hands of partner has since quit job

children.content: Those affected by the murder of Tiffany Spence, who died from horrific injuries inflicted by her partner Adam Britt, tell a sentencing hearing of the long-term impacts of her death.

image.altText: A woman wearing a purple singlet top.

items.title: NSW residents say earthquake near dam sounded like an 'explosion'

children.content: The magnitude-2.9 earthquake was felt in parts of the Snowy Mountains just before 1pm on Sunday, according to Geosceince Australia.

image.altText: Pastel coloured boxes indicating where reports were made near Tumut on a NSW map

items.title: 'Straight to landfill': Compostable packaging but nowhere to compost it

children.content: The ACT mandates reusable, recyclable and compostable packaging at all public events, but nearly all of it ends up in landfill because there is no facility to compost it.

gallery.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

thumbnail.altText: A meal served on a compostable paper plate with wooden cutlery.

items.title: Court hears of 'tragic mistake' that caused fatal Canberra crash

children.content: The daughters of a motorcyclist killed in Canberra's north tried to reach him for their nightly phone call as he lay dying on the road, an ACT court has heard.

image.altText: A man with a beard smiles, wearing a hat that says Harley Davidson, sitting inside an eatery.

items.title: A man fled a 'bounty' in Canberra. Months later, he was dead in Brisbane

children.content: Neil Tracey's death was ruled an overdose in what investigators would call an open and shut case. His family are questioning if police knew about the bounty that led him to flee his hometown.

image.altText: Graphic of a man's smiling face with newspapers articles surrounding it.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, Off Campus driving ice hockey boom in Canberra

children.content: Women now outnumber men as attendees to Canberra Brave ice hockey games — thanks in part to a surge in popularity for the sport inspired by the recent shows Heated Rivalry and Off Campus.

gallery.altText: Two men in tuxedos with their foreheads touching.
Ella Bright and Belmont Cameli intimately close in a lecture theatre in a scene from Off Campus.
People play ice hockey on a rink in a darkened stadium.

items.title: 'Smiling since last night': Community joy after Irankunda's World Cup goal

children.content: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

articles.title: FIFA's hydration break farce the ultimate insult for weary World Cup fans

children.content: Football fans are forced to endure much to keep their love of the beautiful game alive, but FIFA's cynical new "hydration breaks" are stretching the friendship like never before.

image.altText: Socceroos players have a drink during the game against Türkiye

articles.title: Quick hits: Curaçao's special goal and De Oranje brighten up day four

children.content: De Oranje hit the World Cup and in the middle of a 7-1 thumping, Curaçao's lone goal might have meant the most of all. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: Quick hits artwork day four

articles.title: Sweden's Premier League stars shine to cast Tunisia aside

children.content: Sweden dispatches Tunisia after Côte d'Ivoire scores a last-minute winner against Ecuador and Japan finds a late leveller against the Netherlands.

image.altText: Alexander Isak and Viktor Gyokeres run off in celebration arm in arm

items.title: Property Council hits back at claim tax debate was 'submerged by vested interests' — as it happened

children.content: Housing groups have addressed the Senate inquiry into the federal government’s sweeping changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

image.altText: Mike Zorbas stands near window, arms crossed, with trees and buildings visible through window

items.title: Police uncover note, unused life jackets at scene of 'senseless' murder-suicide

children.content: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

image.altText: A boat on a dock, with police tape in front and two officers examining the scene.

items.title: White House UFC event ends with massive upset win in title fight

children.content: US president Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 ends with Justin Gaethje scoring a massive upset win over Ilia Topuria at the White House.

items.title: Outbreak of this Victorian-era disease labelled a 'health disaster and tragedy'

children.content: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

image.altText: A man inside a lab studying a human skull, a skeleton model hangs nearby.

items.title: Cult musician Oliver Tree presumed dead after chopper crash

children.content: Ahead of his Australian tour, the American musician and online personality is reportedly one of six passengers involved in a fatal helicopter collision.

image.altText: man with moustache and long-dark haired mullet stands stony faced in front of scrap heap

items.title: Shackleton's hut could pose explosive risk to Antarctic explorers

children.content: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Ernest Shackleton and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warned.

items.title: Major highway closure hits 100 days as business payouts slammed

children.content: Mayors want the government to provide "adequate" funding for businesses that are closing and experiencing at least 50 per cent downturns due to the failure of Victoria Pass.

children.content: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

items.title: Disgraced former AFL footballer jailed for pool fraud

children.content: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

items.title: Police investigate spectator brawl during country Victorian football match

children.content: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

image.altText: A security camera still of a man punching someone in the crowd at a football game

items.title: Aussie swimming great McKeown weighing up Olympic future

children.content: As she prepares for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games, Kaylee McKeown will not rule out extending her career through to the 2032 Brisbane Olympics.

image.altText: Kaylee McKeown looks to her left at the wall after a swimming race in Brisbane in 2025.

items.title: Mother of Perth girl shot dead in Pakistan 'in extreme shock'

children.content: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

items.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing'

children.content: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

image.altText: Military vehicles carrying DF-26 ballistic missiles during a military parade in China.

items.title: Business groups urge rejection of CGT changes

children.content: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers close up looking serious with Australian flag in background.

items.title: Travellers owed $4.3 million as dream holidays shattered

children.content: Australian travellers who collectively lost millions after a travel agency collapsed are calling for an overhaul of consumer protections in the sector.

image.altText: A man and woman posing fpor a selfie infront of a resort pool

items.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'

children.content: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

image.altText: An Aboriginal flag flying outside of parliament house

items.title: 'Absolutely terrified': Scolyer's doctor reveals fears of experimental therapy

children.content: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

children.content: A contentious proposal to rename most of Homebush to Strathfield drew criticism as an attempt to lift property values.

items.title: Man who killed friend and hid dismembered body in freezer jailed for life

children.content: Justice Paul Smith says Bobby Weaver murdered his friend, 58-year-old David Thornton, "in cold blood".

items.title: 'Potentially still a future': Liberty Bell Bay smelter given another reprieve

children.content: The administrator for the mothballed manganese smelter is continuing to engage with the consortium designated as its preferred buyer, the ABC understands.

items.title: Why Cameron Munster is always Queensland's Origin saviour

children.content: Ever since his first State of Origin match, Queensland has looked to Cameron Munster in times of trouble. Now, with the series on the line at the MCG, his state will ask its greatest modern hero to do so again.

items.title: Vacant, underused land to make way for housing in next phase of Qld land activation

children.content: Three developers will take ownership of 6 hectares of state-owned land in Banyo in Brisbane's north and build townhouses, affordable housing and an aged care facility.
